title: fraxinus excelsior f. angustifolia
intro: Fraxinus excelsior f. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved ash, is a subspecies of the common ash tree, characterized by its slender leaves and typical ash tree features. The narrow-leaved ash has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. This plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, the narrow-leaved ash is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
